instructions.name: create_market
docs: Create a [`Market`](crate::state::Market) for a given token pair.

accounts.name: bids
docs: Accounts are initialized by client,
anchor discriminator is set first when ix exits,

instructions.name: close_market
docs: Close a [`Market`](crate::state::Market) (only
[`close_market_admin`](crate::state::Market::close_market_admin)).

instructions.name: create_open_orders_indexer
docs: Create an [`OpenOrdersIndexer`](crate::state::OpenOrdersIndexer) account.

instructions.name: close_open_orders_indexer
docs: Close an [`OpenOrdersIndexer`](crate::state::OpenOrdersIndexer) account.

instructions.name: create_open_orders_account
docs: Create an [`OpenOrdersAccount`](crate::state::OpenOrdersAccount).

instructions.name: close_open_orders_account
docs: Close an [`OpenOrdersAccount`](crate::state::OpenOrdersAccount).

instructions.name: place_order
docs: Place an order.
Different types of orders have different effects on the order book,
as described in [`PlaceOrderType`](crate::state::PlaceOrderType).
`price_lots` refers to the price in lots: the number of quote lots
per base lot. It is ignored for `PlaceOrderType::Market` orders.
`expiry_timestamp` is a unix timestamp for when this order should
expire. If 0 is passed in, the order will never expire. If the time
is in the past, the instruction is skipped. Timestamps in the future
are reduced to now + 65,535s.
`limit` determines the maximum number of orders from the book to fill,
and can be used to limit CU spent. When the limit is reached, processing
stops and the instruction succeeds.

instructions.name: edit_order
docs: Edit an order.

instructions.name: edit_order_pegged
docs: Edit an order pegged.

instructions.name: place_orders
docs: Place multiple orders

instructions.name: cancel_all_and_place_orders
docs: Cancel orders and place multiple orders.

instructions.name: place_order_pegged
docs: Place an oracle-peg order.

instructions.name: place_take_order
docs: Place an order that shall take existing liquidity off of the book, not
add a new order off the book.
This type of order allows for instant token settlement for the taker.

instructions.name: consume_events
docs: Process up to `limit` [events](crate::state::AnyEvent).
When a user places a 'take' order, they do not know beforehand which
market maker will have placed the 'make' order that they get executed
against. This prevents them from passing in a market maker's
[`OpenOrdersAccount`](crate::state::OpenOrdersAccount), which is needed
to credit/debit the relevant tokens to/from the maker. As such, Openbook
uses a 'crank' system, where `place_order` only emits events, and
`consume_events` handles token settlement.
Currently, there are two types of events: [`FillEvent`](crate::state::FillEvent)s
and [`OutEvent`](crate::state::OutEvent)s.
A `FillEvent` is emitted when an order is filled, and it is handled by
debiting whatever the taker is selling from the taker and crediting
it to the maker, and debiting whatever the taker is buying from the
maker and crediting it to the taker. Note that *no tokens are moved*,
these are just debits and credits to each party's [`Position`](crate::state::Position).
An `OutEvent` is emitted when a limit order needs to be removed from
the book during a `place_order` invocation, and it is handled by
crediting whatever the maker would have sold (quote token in a bid,
base token in an ask) back to the maker.

instructions.name: consume_given_events
docs: Process the [events](crate::state::AnyEvent) at the given positions.

instructions.name: cancel_order
docs: Cancel an order by its `order_id`.
Note that this doesn't emit an [`OutEvent`](crate::state::OutEvent) because a
maker knows that they will be passing in their own [`OpenOrdersAccount`](crate::state::OpenOrdersAccount).

instructions.name: cancel_order_by_client_order_id
docs: Cancel an order by its `client_order_id`.
Note that this doesn't emit an [`OutEvent`](crate::state::OutEvent) because a
maker knows that they will be passing in their own [`OpenOrdersAccount`](crate::state::OpenOrdersAccount).

instructions.name: cancel_all_orders
docs: Cancel up to `limit` orders, optionally filtering by side

instructions.name: deposit
docs: Deposit a certain amount of `base` and `quote` lamports into one's
[`Position`](crate::state::Position).
Makers might wish to `deposit`, rather than have actual tokens moved for
each trade, in order to reduce CUs.

instructions.name: refill
docs: Refill a certain amount of `base` and `quote` lamports. The amount being passed is the
total lamports that the [`Position`](crate::state::Position) will have.
Makers might wish to `refill`, rather than have actual tokens moved for
each trade, in order to reduce CUs.

instructions.name: settle_funds
docs: Withdraw any available tokens.

instructions.name: settle_funds_expired
docs: Withdraw any available tokens when the market is expired (only
[`close_market_admin`](crate::state::Market::close_market_admin)).

instructions.name: sweep_fees
docs: Sweep fees, as a [`Market`](crate::state::Market)'s admin.

instructions.name: set_delegate
docs: Update the [`delegate`](crate::state::OpenOrdersAccount::delegate) of an open orders account.

instructions.name: set_market_expired
docs: Set market to expired before pruning orders and closing the market (only
[`close_market_admin`](crate::state::Market::close_market_admin)).

instructions.name: prune_orders
docs: Remove orders from the book when the market is expired (only
[`close_market_admin`](crate::state::Market::close_market_admin)).

errors.name: InvalidInputNameLength
errors.msg: Name lenght above limit

errors.name: InvalidInputMarketExpired
errors.msg: Market cannot be created as expired

errors.name: InvalidInputMarketFees
errors.msg: Taker fees should be positive and if maker fees are negative, greater or equal to their abs value

errors.name: InvalidInputLots
errors.msg: Lots cannot be negative

errors.name: InvalidInputLotsSize
errors.msg: Lots size above market limits

errors.name: InvalidInputOrdersAmounts
errors.msg: Input amounts above limits

errors.name: InvalidInputCancelSize
errors.msg: Price lots should be greater than zero

errors.name: InvalidInputPriceLots
errors.msg: Expected cancel size should be greater than zero

errors.name: InvalidInputPegLimit
errors.msg: Peg limit should be greater than zero

errors.name: InvalidInputOrderType
errors.msg: The order type is invalid. A taker order must be Market or ImmediateOrCancel

errors.name: InvalidInputOrderId
errors.msg: Order id cannot be zero

errors.name: InvalidInputHeapSlots
errors.msg: Slot above heap limit

errors.name: InvalidOracleTypes
errors.msg: Cannot combine two oracles of different providers

errors.name: InvalidSecondOracle
errors.msg: Cannot configure secondary oracle without primary

errors.name: NoCloseMarketAdmin
errors.msg: This market does not have a `close_market_admin` and thus cannot be closed.

errors.name: InvalidCloseMarketAdmin
errors.msg: The signer of this transaction is not this market's `close_market_admin`.

errors.name: InvalidOpenOrdersAdmin
errors.msg: The `open_orders_admin` required by this market to sign all instructions that creates orders is missing or is not valid

errors.name: InvalidConsumeEventsAdmin
errors.msg: The `consume_events_admin` required by this market to sign all instructions that consume events is missing or is not valid

errors.name: InvalidMarketVault
errors.msg: Provided `market_vault` is invalid

errors.name: IndexerActiveOO
errors.msg: Cannot be closed due to the existence of open orders accounts

errors.name: OraclePegInvalidOracleState
errors.msg: Cannot place a peg order due to invalid oracle state

errors.name: UnknownOracleType
errors.msg: oracle type cannot be determined

errors.name: OracleConfidence
errors.msg: an oracle does not reach the confidence threshold

errors.name: OracleStale
errors.msg: an oracle is stale

errors.name: OrderIdNotFound
errors.msg: Order id not found on the orderbook

errors.name: EventHeapContainsElements
errors.msg: Event heap contains elements and market can't be closed

errors.name: InvalidOrderPostIOC
errors.msg: ImmediateOrCancel is not a PostOrderType

errors.name: InvalidOrderPostMarket
errors.msg: Market is not a PostOrderType

errors.name: WouldSelfTrade
errors.msg: would self trade

errors.name: MarketHasExpired
errors.msg: The Market has already expired.

errors.name: InvalidPriceLots
errors.msg: Price lots should be greater than zero

errors.name: InvalidOraclePrice
errors.msg: Oracle price above market limits

errors.name: MarketHasNotExpired
errors.msg: The Market has not expired yet.

errors.name: NoOwnerOrDelegate
errors.msg: No correct owner or delegate.

errors.name: NoOwner
errors.msg: No correct owner

errors.name: OpenOrdersFull
errors.msg: No free order index in open orders account

errors.name: BookContainsElements
errors.msg: Book contains elements

errors.name: OpenOrdersOrderNotFound
errors.msg: Could not find order in user account

errors.name: InvalidPostAmount
errors.msg: Amount to post above book limits

errors.name: DisabledOraclePeg
errors.msg: Oracle peg orders are not enabled for this market

errors.name: NonEmptyMarket
errors.msg: Cannot close a non-empty market

errors.name: NonEmptyOpenOrdersPosition
errors.msg: Cannot close a non-empty open orders account

errors.name: WouldExecutePartially
errors.msg: Fill-Or-Kill order would generate a partial execution

types.name: NonZeroPubkeyOption
docs: Like `Option`, but implemented for `Pubkey` to be used with `zero_copy`

fields.name: bids_base_lots
docs: Base lots in open bids

fields.name: asks_base_lots
docs: Base lots in open asks

fields.name: penalty_heap_count
docs: Count of ixs when events are added to the heap
To avoid this, send remaining accounts in order to process the events

fields.name: maker_volume
docs: Cumulative maker volume in quote native units (display only)

fields.name: taker_volume
docs: Cumulative taker volume in quote native units (display only)

fields.name: bids_quote_lots
docs: Quote lots in open bids

fields.name: locked_price
docs: Price at which user's assets were locked

types.name: InnerNode
docs: InnerNodes and LeafNodes compose the binary tree of orders.
Each InnerNode has exactly two children, which are either InnerNodes themselves,
or LeafNodes. The children share the top `prefix_len` bits of `key`. The left
child has a 0 in the next bit, and the right a 1.

fields.name: prefix_len
docs: number of highest `key` bits that all children share
e.g. if it's 2, the two highest bits of `key` will be the same on all children

fields.name: key
docs: only the top `prefix_len` bits of `key` are relevant

fields.name: children
docs: indexes into `BookSide::nodes`

fields.name: child_earliest_expiry
docs: The earliest expiry timestamp for the left and right subtrees.
Needed to be able to find and remove expired orders without having to
iterate through the whole bookside.

types.name: LeafNode
docs: LeafNodes represent an order in the binary tree

fields.name: tag
docs: NodeTag

fields.name: owner_slot
docs: Index into the owning OpenOrdersAccount's OpenOrders

fields.name: time_in_force
docs: Time in seconds after `timestamp` at which the order expires.
A value of 0 means no expiry.

fields.name: key
docs: The binary tree key, see new_node_key()

fields.name: owner
docs: Address of the owning OpenOrdersAccount

fields.name: quantity
docs: Number of base lots to buy or sell, always >=1

fields.name: timestamp
docs: The time the order was placed

fields.name: peg_limit
docs: If the effective price of an oracle pegged order exceeds this limit,
it will be considered invalid and may be removed.
Only applicable in the oracle_pegged OrderTree

fields.name: client_order_id
docs: User defined id for this order, used in FillEvents

types.name: OrderTreeNodes
docs: A binary tree on AnyNode::key()
The key encodes the price in the top 64 bits.

types.name: I80F48
docs: Nothing in Rust shall use these types. They only exist so that the Anchor IDL
knows about them and typescript can deserialize it.

types.name: SelfTradeBehavior
docs: Self trade behavior controls how taker orders interact with resting limit orders of the same account.
This setting has no influence on placing a resting or oracle pegged limit order that does not match
immediately, instead it's the responsibility of the user to correctly configure his taker orders.

types.name: SideAndOrderTree
docs: SideAndOrderTree is a storage optimization, so we don't need two bytes for the data

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

fields.name: base_decimals
docs: Number of decimals used for the base token.
Used to convert the oracle's price into a native/native price.

fields.name: time_expiry
docs: No expiry = 0. Market will expire and no trading allowed after time_expiry

fields.name: collect_fee_admin
docs: Admin who can collect fees from the market

fields.name: open_orders_admin
docs: Admin who must sign off on all order creations

fields.name: consume_events_admin
docs: Admin who must sign off on all event consumptions

fields.name: close_market_admin
docs: Admin who can set market expired, prune orders and close the market

fields.name: name
docs: Name. Trailing zero bytes are ignored.

fields.name: bids
docs: Address of the BookSide account for bids

fields.name: asks
docs: Address of the BookSide account for asks

fields.name: event_heap
docs: Address of the EventHeap account

fields.name: oracle_a
docs: Oracles account address

fields.name: oracle_config
docs: Oracle configuration

fields.name: quote_lot_size
docs: Number of quote native in a quote lot. Must be a power of 10.
Primarily useful for increasing the tick size on the market: A lot price
of 1 becomes a native price of quote_lot_size/base_lot_size becomes a
ui price of quote_lot_size*base_decimals/base_lot_size/quote_decimals.

fields.name: base_lot_size
docs: Number of base native in a base lot. Must be a power of 10.
Example: If base decimals for the underlying asset is 6, base lot size
is 100 and and base position lots is 10_000 then base position native is
1_000_000 and base position ui is 1.

fields.name: seq_num
docs: Total number of orders seen

fields.name: registration_time
docs: Timestamp in seconds that the market was registered at.

fields.name: maker_fee
docs: Fees
Fee (in 10^-6) when matching maker orders.
maker_fee < 0 it means some of the taker_fees goes to the maker
maker_fee > 0, it means no taker_fee to the maker, and maker fee goes to the referral

fields.name: taker_fee
docs: Fee (in 10^-6) for taker orders, always >= 0.

fields.name: fees_accrued
docs: Total fees accrued in native quote

fields.name: fees_to_referrers
docs: Total fees settled in native quote

fields.name: referrer_rebates_accrued
docs: Referrer rebates to be distributed

fields.name: fees_available
docs: Fees generated and available to withdraw via sweep_fees

fields.name: maker_volume
docs: Cumulative maker volume (same as taker volume) in quote native units

fields.name: taker_volume_wo_oo
docs: Cumulative taker volume in quote native units due to place take orders

types.name: EventHeap
docs: Container for the different EventTypes.
Events are stored in a fixed-array of nodes. Free nodes are connected by a single-linked list
starting at free_head while used nodes form a circular doubly-linked list starting at
used_head.
